name: Jeanne Lee
abstract: Jeanne Lee (January 29, 1939 – October 25, 2000) was an American jazz singer, poet and composer. Best known for a wide range of vocal styles she mastered, Lee collaborated with numerous distinguished composers and performers who included Gunter Hampel, Andrew Cyrille, Ran Blake, Carla Bley, Anthony Braxton, Marion Brown, Archie Shepp, Ma…

about_quotes.speaker: Ran Blake
about_quotes.evidence: I think one of the best things we did is our first album, “Laura.” ... Jeanne and I would dig more of the Silvers ... we did it at a house party in Stockholm and it got to be something that we would occasionally do as an encore.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ran Blake interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 16, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I first was with Jeanne Lee, that was the first New York job I ever had.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001
